column6: There are total 3 rounds in the hiring process. The first round consists of two sections-Cognitive & Technical Assessment and Coding Round. There were a mix of questions from Cognitive Section which includes English Ability, Critical Reasoning & Problem Solving, Abstract Reasoning and from Technical Section which includes Common Application & MS Office, Pseudocode, Network Security & Cloud. There were 90 questions (Cognitive Section-50 questions and Technical Section-40 questions) to be solved in 90 minutes. This is an elimination round and only after you clear this round you'll proceed to the coding round. There were 2 questions to be solved in 45 minutes and you are free to choose any programming language of your choice. Both the sections are attempted one after the other on the same day. Second round is Communication Assessment which consists of questions from Reading (8 questions), Listening/Repeat (16 questions), Q&A (24 questions), Sentence Jumble/Sentence Builds (10 questions), Story Telling (3 questions), Speaking (2 questions). There were 63 questions to be solved in approximately 30 minutes. Each question in each section is timed. This is not an elimination round but is mandatory to attempt.
column7:
The third round was basically an HR Interview Round. It lasts for about 20 minutes. Some of the questions asked by the interviewer are listed below:
1. Introduce yourself
2. Tell me about your projects in brief
3. Tell me some of the problems faced during projects
4. How did you handle any conflicts during projects
5. How many members were there in your team and what was your role in the project
6. Tell me about any one of your achievements that you are proud of 
7. What are your strengths and weaknesses
8. Do you have any certifications
9. What is your most favourite and least favourite subject
10. Do I have any questions for them
column8: They didn't ask any technical questions.
column9: You are free to choose any programming language of your choice.
